---
title: "Motivation 1 — Sparse-constrained GLM"
subtitle: "The problem we want to solve"
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
*Draft outline — content to come.*
:::

## What sparse-constrained GLM is

- The GLM setup: response $y$, design $X$, log-likelihood $\ell(\beta_0, \beta; \mathcal{D})$.
- The cardinality constraint: $\|\beta\|_0 \le k$.
- Why this problem appears everywhere: GWAS, gene-expression classification, bodyfat-style applied regression.

## The optimisation we are trying to solve

$$
\min_{\beta_0,\,\beta}\;
-\frac{1}{n}\,\ell(\beta_0, \beta;\,\mathcal{D})
\;+\;
\lambda\,\|\beta\|_2^2
\qquad
\text{subject to}\quad \|\beta\|_0 \le k.
$$

## Why this is hard

- Combinatorial in $\binom{p}{k}$.
- NP-hard in general.
- Existing approaches: lasso (next page), MIO (page after).

---

[Next → Lasso](02-lasso.qmd)
